title: Review code for cryptographic misuse
description: Audit a change for the common misuse patterns: weak modes, nonce reuse, bad hashes, custom crypto, and timing leaks.
body: Steps: 1. Find every crypto call and identify the construction and parameters. 2. Flag unauthenticated modes (ECB/raw CBC/CTR), missing tag verification, and hand-rolled ciphers/MACs. 3. Trace nonce/IV sources for reuse and randomness sources for non-CSPRNG use. 4. Check password storage for memory-hard salted KDFs and secret comparisons for constant time. 5. Confirm keys are managed (no hardcoded keys, rotation possible). Rollback point: before merging any change touching secrets. Validation: every finding maps to a named safer construction and tamper/timing tests pass; a clean self-test of one's own ciphertext is not evidence of security and must not end the review.
